title: Novenia acaulis
taxonomy: Novenia acaulis is classified within the family of plants known as Sapindaceae. This family consists of flowering plants commonly referred to as soapberry or lychee family. Novenia acaulis is a genus of flowering plants that is endemic to New Caledonia. The classification of Novenia acaulis is further divided into a single species, Novenia acaulis. The species is part of the order Sapindales in the plant kingdom.

intro: Novenia acaulis, also known as Mountain Pepper, is a flowering plant species native to the Australian Alps. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in reducing pain and preventing infections. The bioactive constituents of Novenia acaulis include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, contributing to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, Novenia acaulis is used to make herbal preparations like teas, tinctures, and salves, which are commonly used to treat minor wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues.
